channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: Indian Navy Seeks MANTA Drone: Autonomous Ship-Launched Naval ISR and Targeting System
item.description: The Indian Navy has outlined an ambitious requirement under the DISC 14 innovation challenge for the development of a Ship-Launched Multi-Domain Autonomous Naval Tactical Attack Drone, designated as the MANTA Drone. The system is intended to fill a critical capability gap in long-range, persistent maritime surveillance and targeting, particularly in contested and high-risk environments near adversary coastlines.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Airbus A400M Too Costly? IAF’s Medium Transport Aircraft Race Narrows to C-390 and C-130J
item.description: The competition for the Indian Air Force’s Medium Transport Aircraft (MTA) program has entered a final phase following the Defence Acquisition Council’s (DAC) Acceptance of Necessity (AoN) in March 2026 for the procurement of 60 aircraft. The program is intended to replace the aging An-32 and partially supplement the Il-76 fleet, marking one of the most significant airlift capability upgrades for the IAF in decades.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India’s Hypersonic Trident: How Dhvani, LR-AShM, and ET-LDHCM Are Reshaping Strategic Deterrence
item.description: India’s transition into the hypersonic weapons domain is no longer aspirational—it is structurally underway. What is emerging is not a single missile program, but a layered hypersonic strike ecosystem that spans deterrence, sea denial, and conventional precision strike. At the core of this transformation are three distinct but interlinked systems: the Dhvani Hypersonic Glide Vehicle (HGV), the Long-Range Anti-Ship Hypersonic Missile (LR-AShM), and the Extended Trajectory Long Duration Hypersonic Cruise Missile (ET-LDHCM).
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India’s Hypersonic Defense Paradox: Building Interceptors for Weapons That Barely Exist
item.description: As India accelerates its hypersonic weapons programs, it is simultaneously investing in counter-hypersonic defenses—creating a paradox that reflects the evolving nature of modern warfare. In 2026, DRDO initiated work on hypersonic interceptors designed to neutralize incoming high-speed threats.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: The Hardened Triad: Transitioning to Silo-Based Deterrence and Canisterized Readiness
item.description: India’s nuclear doctrine has long been anchored in restraint, survivability, and credibility. The “No First Use” (NFU) policy, combined with a recessed deterrence posture, has historically shaped the architecture of its Strategic Forces Command (SFC). However, developments observed through 2025 and into 2026 indicate a subtle but consequential shift—one that does not abandon NFU, but materially reinforces it through infrastructure, readiness, and deployment philosophy.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India’s IBG Revolution: How AI-Enabled Mountain Strike Units Are Rewriting Warfare Along the LAC
item.description: The Indian Army is in the midst of a transformation that is arguably more consequential than any single weapons acquisition program. It is structural, doctrinal, and technological all at once. The operationalization of Integrated Battle Groups (IBGs), now moving from concept to field reality in 2026, marks the most radical overhaul of India’s land warfare philosophy since independence. Unlike previous reforms that focused on incremental modernization, the IBG model redefines how the Army fights, moves, and makes decisions under combat conditions—particularly along the Line of Actual Control (LAC), where terrain, time, and information dominance are decisive.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: The Unspoken Calculus of Restraint: Why India Forged No Combat Rescue for Wing Commander Abhinandan Varthaman Amid the 2019 Nuclear Shadow
item.description: In the frenzied hours following the 27 February 2019 aerial clash over the Line of Control, where Wing Commander Abhinandan Varthaman’s MiG-21 Bison was brought down after reportedly downing a Pakistani F-16, the absence of any visible Indian combat search-and-rescue (CSAR) effort has fueled persistent online discourse.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Pakistan Navy “was poised to sink Indian Navy Aircraft Carrier : Pakistani Navy Chief
item.description: he Pakistan Navy inducted on Saturday another Turkish-made MILGEM Class Corvette into its fleet in the country’s port city of Karachi, official statements said. The 2nd PN MILGEM Class Corvette, PNS Khaibar, was inducted into the country’s naval fleet during a ceremony attended by Naval Chief Admiral Naveed Ashraf and other senior officials, according to an official statement.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Pak would hit Kolkata in ‘future’ conflict: Khawaja Asif
item.description: Pakistan’s Defence Minister Khawaja Asif on Saturday warned India that it would respond with a strike in Kolkata to any “future misadventures.” “If India tries to stage any false flag operation this time, then God-willingly, we will take it to Kolkata,” Asif said while talking to reporters at his hometown of Sialkot, some 130kms from Lahore.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: ISRO eyes May launch; Artemis-II will be a success, says Chairman V Narayanan
item.description: ISRO is working towards its next launch in May, with several missions lined up in the new financial year, Chairman V Narayanan said on Saturday. Narayanan spoke to reporters after receiving the KPP Nambiar Award 2025, instituted by the Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE) Kerala.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Naxalism, Kashmir Militancy See Major Decline Over 12 Years: Report
item.description: India has recorded a significant decline in two of its most persistent internal security challenges — Left Wing Extremism (LWE) and militancy in Jammu and Kashmir — over the past 12 years, with sustained security operations, better intelligence coordination and development-led interventions playing a decisive role, according to a report.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Modi hails maritime sector’s contribution on National Maritime Day
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Sunday highlighted India’s maritime heritage and lauded the contribution of those associated with the sector on National Maritime Day.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: UP Man Arrested On Suspicion Of Terror Involvement, Had ISIS Links Decade Ago
item.description: The Delhi Police’s Special Cell has detained a youth from Uttar Pradesh’s Kushinagar district on suspicion of involvement in terrorist activities and taken him to the national capital, officials said on Sunday. According to Uttar Pradesh police, the youth, Rizwan Ahmad, of Chhawani area of Padrauna town, came under the scanner during the investigation of a case registered by the Special Cell.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Over 6,000 Uttar Pradesh workers in Israel safe; govt in constant touch: Officials
item.description: Over 6,000 workers from Uttar Pradesh currently in Israel are safe despite the prevailing tense situation, with the state government maintaining continuous contact with Indian authorities on the ground, officials said on Sunday. Principal Secretary Labour and Employment Department, Uttar Pradesh, Shanmuga Sundaram, is in regular contact with the Indian Embassy in Israel to track developments and ensure the safety of Indian nationals, particularly those from the state.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: 18 flights per day at Leh airport to boost tourism, says Ladakh L-G
item.description: Flights at Leh airport have increased from eight to 18 per day to boost air connectivity ahead of the peak tourism season, Lieutenant Governor Vinai Kumar Saxena said on Sunday. He said two more flights are expected to begin operations soon, providing flexibility for visitors to plan their journeys, and invited holiday-makers to experience Ladakh for an “unforgettable” trip.
item.dc:creator: admin
